title: Calibrate and preserve retrieval scores for existing-node reuse
problem: During ag95-diverse-003-16p, most allowed_existing_nodes were weak topical hits and the model repeatedly noted that candidates were unrelated. The package also strips scores from allowed_existing_nodes, so the prompt cannot distinguish strong reuse candidates from low-score noise.
proposed_change: Preserve retrieval scores and match rationales in allowed_existing_nodes, add a minimum-score or top-k-by-kind policy, and evaluate a simple reranker that favors title/alias overlap and paper-topic proximity before presenting reusable nodes to Codex.
acceptance_criteria: Paper packages include scores for allowed existing nodes; low-score candidates are either filtered or labeled as weak; audit reports reuse-candidate score distributions; a diverse pilot shows fewer unrelated-candidate notes without reducing real cross-paper reuse.

observations.context: In the 8 new-paper extension, the model notes for 6 of 8 deltas said allowed/local search candidates were unrelated or not reused. Package local_search_results retained very low scores around 0.03-0.05, while allowed_existing_nodes stripped the scores before prompting. mr4792069 did find real P=W candidates, but also at low scores, so calibration must preserve recall while labeling weak hits.
